refined_tasks.original_title: Mobile-first responsive redesign with native touch interactions
refined_tasks.title: Implement mobile-first responsive design with touch gestures and bottom navigation
refined_tasks.description:
**Dependencies:** ```bash cd ui npm install react-swipeable ``` **Global CSS (ui/src/index.css):** Add to top of file: ```css /* Mobile-first optimizations */ * { touch-action: manipulation; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; } html, body { overflow-x: hidden; max-width: 100vw; } /* Prevent iOS zoom on input focus */ input, select, textarea { font-size: 16px; } ``` **Dashboard Grid (ui/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx):** Line 104 - Change grid from: ```tsx
``` to: ```tsx
``` **Create Mobile Navigation (ui/src/components/MobileNav.tsx):** ```tsx import { Link, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'; import { Home, Users, CheckSquare, DollarSign, Settings } from 'lucide-react'; export default function MobileNav() { const location = useLocation(); const isActive = (path: string) => location.pathname === path; return (
Home Tasks Costs Agents
); } ``` **Update Layout (ui/src/App.tsx or Layout component):** After main content div, add: ```tsx import MobileNav from './components/MobileNav'; // In JSX:
{/* existing content */}
``` **Task Row Swipe Gestures (ui/src/components/TaskRow.tsx or Dashboard.tsx):** If TaskRow is a separate component, modify it. Otherwise, add to task rendering in Dashboard.tsx: ```tsx import { useSwipeable } from 'react-swipeable'; function TaskRow({ task, onMarkDone, onReassign }) { const handlers = useSwipeable({ onSwipedLeft: () => onMarkDone(task.id), onSwipedRight: () => onReassign(task.id), trackMouse: false, // disable on desktop preventScrollOnSwipe: true, delta: 50, // minimum swipe distance }); return (
{/* existing task content */}
); } ``` **Agent Grid Mobile Layout:** Find agent grid (likely in Dashboard.tsx or Agents.tsx), change from: ```tsx
``` to: ```tsx
``` **Forms - Native Mobile Inputs:** Update any date/number inputs to use native controls: ```tsx {/* Date picker */} {/* Number input */} ``` **Testing Checklist (document in PR/task):** 1. Chrome DevTools responsive mode: - Test at 375px (iPhone SE) - Test at 390px (iPhone 12/13/14) - Test at 360px (Android) 2. Real device testing: - iOS Safari (15+) - Android Chrome (latest) 3. Landscape mode (both orientations) 4. Touch interactions: - Swipe left/right on tasks - Tap precision on bottom nav - No double-tap zoom on buttons 5. No horizontal scroll at any breakpoint **Files to Modify:** - ui/src/index.css (add mobile optimizations) - ui/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx (grid layout, task swipe) - ui/src/components/MobileNav.tsx (new file) - ui/src/App.tsx (add MobileNav, update layout padding) - Any date/number input components (native input types) **Acceptance Criteria:** - All pages render at 375px width without horizontal scroll (test in Chrome DevTools) - Dashboard cards stack to 1 column on mobile (<640px) - Task rows respond to swipe left (mark done) and swipe right (reassign) on mobile devices (disable trackMouse to avoid desktop interference) - Agent grid shows 1 column mobile, 2 columns tablet (640px+), 3 columns desktop (1024px+) - Bottom navigation bar visible only on mobile (<640px), hidden on desktop - Bottom nav highlights active route with amber color - Forms use native mobile date picker and number pad (inputMode="numeric") - No double-tap zoom on buttons/inputs (verified by touch-action CSS) - Tested on iOS Safari 15+ and Android Chrome without layout breaks - Main content has 64px bottom padding on mobile to prevent overlap with fixed nav

tech_decisions: **Framework Stack:** React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS for frontend, Node.js + Express + SQLite for backend. **Real-time:** WebSocket (ws + reconnecting-websocket) for bidirectional updates. **Charts:** Recharts for declarative, responsive visualizations. **Drag-and-Drop:** react-beautiful-dnd for task reordering, ReactFlow for dependency graphs. **Mobile:** Mobile-first CSS (Tailwind breakpoints), react-swipeable for touch gestures. **Data Fetching:** React Query v5 with WebSocket invalidation for cache synchronization. **Routing:** React Router v6 for client-side navigation. **Build:** Vite for fast development and optimized production builds.
